nodes.description: White/red light activating phytochromes and CRYs
Warm vs cool ambient temperature; warm stabilises PIF4
Vernalization signal inducing VIN3 to silence FLC
Photosynthetic sugar; represses miR156 to drive phase change
Allelic activator of FLC; kept constitutive in this model
BR co-receptor; treated as constitutive scaffold
F-box of SCF complex that ubiquitinates DELLAs; constitutive
bHLH that heterodimerises with PIFs to block DNA binding
Rate-limiting ABA biosynthesis enzyme
Composite of ACS (ACC synthase) + ACO (ACC oxidase) producing ethylene
Juvenile-phase miRNA targeting SPL TFs
Bioactive GAs (GA1/GA4); trigger DELLA degradation via GID1
Brassinolide; growth-promoting steroid hormone
Principal natural auxin driving cell elongation
Adenine-based cell-division-promoting hormone
Gaseous hormone; inhibits cell elongation via EIN3
Stress hormone; stabilises ABI5 and represses growth
Composite of GA20ox1/2/3; late GA biosynthesis
Composite of GA3ox1/2; final activation step to bioactive GA
Composite of GA2ox1/2; GA inactivation / catabolism
Composite of BR biosynthesis enzymes
Composite of auxin biosynthesis enzymes YUC8 and TAA1
Composite of IPT3/5/7 isopentenyltransferases; CK biosynthesis
Cytokinin degradation enzyme
Composite GA receptor (3 paralogs)
Composite DELLA repressor; master growth inhibitor
Membrane receptor kinase for BR
GSK3-like kinase that phosphorylates/inactivates BZR1/BES1
Phosphatase that dephosphorylates and inactivates BIN2
Composite BR-regulated master TF
Auxin co-receptor targeting Aux/IAAs for degradation
Aux/IAA repressor that binds and inhibits ARFs
Composite of growth-promoting auxin response factors
Red-light photoreceptor; inhibits PIFs and stabilises HY5
bHLH that blocks PIF function in shade
Composite growth-promoting PIFs
Cytokinin receptor (representative of AHK2/3/4)
Histidine phosphotransfer protein in CK signaling
Type-B response regulator TF for cytokinin response
Ethylene receptor; active (signalling) when ethylene absent
Raf-like kinase; inhibits EIN2 in absence of ethylene
Central positive regulator of ethylene response
Master TF for ethylene response; limits growth
ABA receptors that bind ABA and inhibit PP2Cs
PP2C phosphatase; inhibits SnRK2 in absence of ABA
SNF1-related kinase activating ABA responses
bZIP TF; stabilised by SnRK2; growth-repressive
Cold-induced chromatin factor; silences FLC
MADS-box flowering repressor that also delays growth transition
Florigen; promotes flowering transition
Adult-phase master TF in age pathway
Clock-regulated photoperiod hub activating FKF1/CO/FT
Blue-light F-box that stabilises CO (degrades CDF1)
B-box TF; day-length-gated FT activator
Composite of autonomous-pathway FLC repressors
Polycomb Repressive Complex 2 silencing FLC after vernalization
Adult-phase miRNA targeting AP2-like TFs
Composite AP2-like FT repressors (miR172 targets)
MADS-box FT repressor; stabilised at cool ambient temp
MADS-box paralogue of SVP; temperature-responsive FT repressor
Evening Complex; circadian repressor of PIF4
Composite of SL biosynthesis enzymes
Branching-inhibiting carotenoid-derived hormone
Composite floral-integrator TFs downstream of FT
E3 ligase; targets HY5 for degradation in the dark
bZIP TF promoting photomorphogenesis / short hypocotyl
Catalyses T6P synthesis from UDP-glucose and G6P
Sugar status signal activating FT and repressing miR156
Dephosphorylates BZR1/BES1 - constitutive BR-signalling activator
O-fucosyltransferase activating DELLAs
Final aerial shoot height at bolting/flowering stage

edges.mechanism: Sucrose (and its hydrolytic hexose products glucose/fructose) represses MIR156A and MIR156C gene transcription, reducing mature miR156 levels to promote juvenile-to-adult transition

evidence.title: Sugar promotes vegetative phase change in Arabidopsis thaliana by repressing the expression of MIR156A and MIR156C
evidence.authors: Li Yang, Mingli Xu, Yeonjong Koo, Jia He, R Scott Poethig
evidence.evidence_sentence: Glucose and fructose repressed the accumulation of miR156 by about 50%; exogenous sucrose significantly accelerates abaxial trichome production in wild-type plants
evidence.claim: Sucrose (and hexoses) directly repress MIR156A and MIR156C expression

evidence.title: Sugar is an endogenous cue for juvenile-to-adult phase transition in plants
evidence.authors: Shuai Yu, Liang Cao, Chuan-Miao Zhou, Tian-Qi Zhang
evidence.evidence_sentence: The abundance of pri-MIR156A and pri-MIR156C was greatly reduced after 1 day of treatment with 50 mM sucrose; mature miR156 was decreased after 1 day of sugar treatment
evidence.claim: Sucrose represses MIR156A and MIR156C transcription

edges.mechanism: miR156 targets SPL9 mRNA for cleavage and translational repression; high miR156 in juvenile phase keeps SPL9 levels low

evidence.title: The sequential action of miR156 and miR172 regulates developmental timing in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Gang Wu, Mee Yeon Park, Susan R Conway, Jia-Wei Wang, Detlef Weigel, R Scott Poethig
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR156 represses SPL2, SPL3, SPL4, SPL5, SPL6, SPL9, SPL10, SPL11, SPL13, SPL15 via microRNA cleavage/translational repression

evidence.title: Sugar promotes vegetative phase change in Arabidopsis thaliana by repressing the expression of MIR156A and MIR156C
evidence.authors: Li Yang, Mingli Xu, Yeonjong Koo, Jia He, R Scott Poethig
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sugar treatment with sucrose greatly reduced pri-MIR156A and pri-MIR156C abundance and mature miR156 levels, while transcript levels of miR156-targeted genes SPL9 and SPL15 were markedly increased
evidence.claim: miR156 post-transcriptionally represses SPL9; when miR156 decreases, SPL9 increases

edges.mechanism: SPL9 promotes floral transition and shoot maturation; higher SPL9 activity leads to earlier flowering and shorter inflorescence stems. spl9 spl15 double mutant showed significantly reduced inflorescence height (6.9 cm vs 12.1 cm in wild-type) demonstrating SPL9 shortens plant height via earlier reproductive transition

evidence.title: The microRNA regulated SBP-box genes SPL9 and SPL15 control shoot maturation in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Stefan Schwarz, Arne V Grande, Nora Bujdoso, Heinz Saedler, Peter Huijser
evidence.evidence_sentence: Inflorescence height was measured between the rosette and the first flower; double mutant showed significantly reduced height (6.9 cm vs 12.1 cm in wild-type); double mutant bolted and flowered somewhat later than wild-type (bolting 18.5 DAS vs 16.3 DAS)
evidence.claim: SPL9 promotes shoot maturation; loss of SPL9 and SPL15 reduces inflorescence height and delays flowering

edges.mechanism: Extended cold exposure progressively induces VIN3 transcription via unknown cold sensors

evidence.title: The Arabidopsis thaliana vernalization response requires a polycomb-like protein complex that also includes VERNALIZATION INSENSITIVE 3
evidence.authors: Wood CC, Robertson M, Tanner G, Peacock WJ, Dennis ES, Helliwell CA
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: VIN3 mRNA is present at very low abundance during growth at warm temperatures, with expression increasing progressively during a vernalization treatment

edges.mechanism: VIN3 PHD protein joins PRC2-like complex to deposit H3K27me3 at FLC and silence it

evidence.title: The Arabidopsis thaliana vernalization response requires a polycomb-like protein complex that also includes VERNALIZATION INSENSITIVE 3
evidence.authors: Wood CC, Robertson M, Tanner G, Peacock WJ, Dennis ES, Helliwell CA
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Loss of VIN3 function prevents loss of H3Ac and methylation of H3K9 and H3K27 in vernalized plants

edges.mechanism: FRI (FRIGIDA) is a coiled-coil protein that recruits chromatin modifiers to activate FLC expression to high levels

evidence.title: The FRIGIDA gene encodes a protein required for elevated FLC expression in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Johanson U, West J, Lister C, Michaels S, Amasino R, Dean C
evidence.evidence_sentence: FRI is required for high levels of FLC expression and confers the late-flowering winter-annual habit

edges.mechanism: FLC (MADS-box transcription factor) directly represses FT expression by binding to its promoter/regulatory regions, delaying flowering

evidence.title: Research progress on the autonomous flowering time pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Jing-Zhi Cheng, Yu-Ping Zhou, Tian-Xiao Lv, Chu-Ping Xie, Chang-En Tian
evidence.journal: Physiology and Molecular Biology of Plants
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLC encodes a MADS box protein that functions as a central repressor of flowering time in Arabidopsis; FLC directly represses FT and SOC1 expression

edges.mechanism: Red light converts phyB to active Pfr form; far-red converts back to inactive Pr

evidence.title: Auxin-Dependent Cell Elongation During the Shade Avoidance Response
evidence.authors: Ma L, Li G
evidence.evidence_sentence: High R:FR light activates phyB (Pfr form)

edges.mechanism: Low R:FR (shade) transcriptionally induces HFR1 via phytochromes as a negative-feedback brake

evidence.title: Inhibition of the shade avoidance response by formation of non-DNA binding bHLH heterodimers
evidence.authors: Hornitschek P, Lorrain S, Zoete V, Michielin O, Fankhauser C
evidence.evidence_sentence: HFR1 (long hypocotyl in FR light) is transcriptionally induced by shade and inhibits this response; HFR1 is induced in a phytochrome-regulated manner

edges.mechanism: Light-activated phyB binds PIF4 to promote its phosphorylation-dependent degradation

evidence.title: The epidermis coordinates thermoresponsive growth through the phyB-PIF4-auxin pathway
evidence.authors: Kim S, Hwang G, Kim S, Thi TN, Kim H, Jeong J, Kim J, Kim J, Choi G, Oh E
evidence.evidence_sentence: phyB directly interacts with PIF4 to promote PIF4 phosphorylation and degradation, repressing thermoresponsive growth

edges.mechanism: HFR1 forms non-DNA-binding heterodimers with PIF4, blocking PIF4 DNA binding without degradation

evidence.title: Inhibition of the shade avoidance response by formation of non-DNA binding bHLH heterodimers
evidence.authors: Hornitschek P, Lorrain S, Zoete V, Michielin O, Fankhauser C
evidence.evidence_sentence: HFR1 directly inhibits these PIFs by forming non-DNA-binding heterodimers with PIF4 and PIF5

edges.mechanism: PAR1 small HLH prevents PIF4 from binding DNA

evidence.title: Inhibition of the shade avoidance response by formation of non-DNA binding bHLH heterodimers
evidence.authors: Hornitschek P, Lorrain S, Zoete V, Michielin O, Fankhauser C
evidence.evidence_sentence: PAR1 and PAR2 are negative regulators of the shade avoidance response, which may also act by preventing other bHLH proteins from binding to DNA. The mechanism of action of PAR1 is apparently similar to that of HFR1

edges.mechanism: High ambient temperature increases PIF4 protein abundance and activity, independent of light

evidence.title: High temperature-mediated adaptations in plant architecture require the bHLH transcription factor PIF4
evidence.authors: Koini MA, Alvey L, Allen T, Tilley CA, Harberd NP, Whitelam GC, Franklin KA
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 is required for thermomorphogenic elongation of hypocotyls and petioles at elevated ambient temperature

edges.mechanism: GA 20-oxidase 1 catalyzes the oxidation of GA53/GA12 through GA44/GA15 and GA19/GA24 to GA20/GA9, a key late step in bioactive GA synthesis

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: The GA20ox overexpression lines display early flowering and increased stem growth phenotypes, whereas GA2ox overexpression lines show late flowering and dwarf phenotypes
evidence.claim: GA20ox1 is a rate-limiting enzyme in GA biosynthesis; its overexpression increases stem height

evidence.title: Overexpression of a gibberellin 20-oxidase gene from Arabidopsis thaliana increases plant height and improves shoot fresh weight
evidence.authors: Huang S, Raman AS, Ream JE, Fujiwara H, Cerny RE, Brown SM
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of Arabidopsis GA20ox1 in transgenic plants increases bioactive GA levels, stem height, and shoot fresh weight

edges.mechanism: GA 3-oxidase 1 (GA4) catalyzes the final step converting GA9/GA20 to bioactive GA4/GA1

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA3ox1 is expressed throughout all stages of plant development, and GA3ox2 is mainly expressed in germinating seeds and vegetative tissues
evidence.claim: GA3ox1 produces bioactive GA; ga3ox1 mutants have reduced stem height

edges.mechanism: GA 2-oxidase 1 inactivates bioactive GAs by 2-beta hydroxylation; reduces GA pool

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: the GA catabolism genes GA2ox1 and GA2ox2 are up-regulated by GA treatment
evidence.claim: GA2ox1 deactivates GAs; GA2ox1 OE produces dwarf plants

edges.mechanism: Bioactive GA feedback-represses GA20ox1 transcription via DELLA degradation; loss of DELLA derepresses GA biosynthesis feedback

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: transcript levels of GA3ox1, GA20ox1, GA20ox2 and GA20ox3 are down-regulated by applied GA
evidence.claim: GA represses GA20ox1 transcription as negative feedback

edges.mechanism: Bioactive GA feedback-represses GA3ox1 transcription via DELLA degradation

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: transcript levels of GA3ox1, GA20ox1, GA20ox2 and GA20ox3 are down-regulated by applied GA
evidence.claim: GA represses GA3ox1 transcription as feedback

edges.mechanism: Bioactive GA upregulates GA2ox1 transcription, promoting its own catabolism (feedforward/homeostasis)

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: the GA catabolism genes GA2ox1 and GA2ox2 are up-regulated by GA treatment
evidence.claim: GA upregulates GA2ox1 as homeostatic feedforward

edges.mechanism: Bioactive GA binds the GID1A receptor, causing conformational change and recruitment of DELLA proteins

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: GID1s bind to bioactive GAs with high affinity, but not to inactive GA derivatives
evidence.claim: GA binds GID1A as soluble receptor; activates downstream signaling

edges.mechanism: GA-bound GID1A physically interacts with DELLA protein RGA, promoting its recognition by SCF-SLY1 and degradation via the 26S proteasome

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA induces rapid degradation of this GA signaling repressor through the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway; SLY1 and GID2 appear to activate GA signaling by targeting GA signaling repressors, DELLA proteins, for ubiquitination and degradation

edges.mechanism: SLY1 is the F-box subunit of an SCF E3 ubiquitin ligase that polyubiquitinates DELLA proteins including RGA, targeting them for 26S proteasome degradation

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLY1 and GID2 appear to activate GA signaling by targeting GA signaling repressors, DELLA proteins, for ubiquitination and degradation
evidence.claim: SLY1 F-box ubiquitinates RGA for proteasome degradation

edges.mechanism: DELLA RGA physically interacts with PIF4 and prevents it from binding target promoters, limiting PIF4-driven growth genes

evidence.title: Interactions between DELLA and growth-regulatory factors to control flowering time and stem elongation
evidence.authors: de Lucas M, Daviere JM, Rodriguez-Falcon M, Pontin M, Iglesias-Pedraz JM, Lorrain S, Fankhauser C, Blazquez MA, Titarenko E, Prat S
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA proteins directly bind to PIF4 and prevent its transcriptional activity; PIF4 overexpression partially rescues the dwarf phenotypes of ga1 and gai-1
evidence.claim: RGA binds and inhibits PIF4 activity

edges.mechanism: DELLA RGA binds BZR1 to prevent BZR1 DNA binding, integrating GA and BR signaling at target genes

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: Under low GA conditions, BES1, BZR1, PIF4, and ARF6 are inhibited by DELLAs
evidence.claim: RGA inhibits BZR1 activity, integrating GA-BR signaling

edges.mechanism: DELLA RGA binds ARF6, inhibiting auxin-driven elongation gene expression

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: Under low GA conditions, BES1, BZR1, PIF4, and ARF6 are inhibited by DELLAs

edges.mechanism: DELLA protein RGA represses cell elongation in stem; rga loss-of-function rescues ga1-3 dwarf phenotype; RGA is the principal DELLA limiting vegetative growth

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: Removing both RGA and GAI functions in the ga1-3 background completely rescues vegetative growth and floral initiation
evidence.claim: RGA represses vegetative growth and stem height

edges.mechanism: DWF4 (CYP90B1) C-22 hydroxylase catalyzes the rate-limiting step in BR biosynthesis

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: DWF4 encodes a C-22 hydroxylase that catalyzes a rate-determining step in BR biosynthesis
evidence.claim: DWF4 is rate-limiting in BR biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Active BZR1 binds BRRE element in DWF4 promoter and represses BR biosynthesis (feedback homeostasis)

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: In the transcriptional regulation of BR biosynthetic genes such as DWF4 and CPD, the activity of BZR1 is primarily responsible for the feedback regulation

edges.mechanism: Brassinolide binds BRI1 leucine-rich repeat receptor kinase ectodomain as a molecular glue, triggering BRI1-BAK1 heterodimerization

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: BL acts as a molecular glue, promoting the association between BRI1 and BAK1
evidence.claim: BR binds BRI1 receptor to initiate signaling

edges.mechanism: BRI1-BAK1 complex phosphorylates and activates BSK1 (BR signaling kinase)

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: BR SIGNALING KINASES (BSKs) activate the phosphatase BRI1-SUPPRESSOR1 (BSU1)

edges.mechanism: BRI1-BAK1 complex phosphorylates and activates BSK1 (BR signaling kinase)

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: BR SIGNALING KINASES (BSKs) activate the phosphatase BRI1-SUPPRESSOR1 (BSU1)

edges.mechanism: BSU1 dephosphorylates BIN2 at pTyr200, inactivating the kinase

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: BSU1 dephosphorylates BIN2

edges.mechanism: BIN2 (a GSK3-like kinase) phosphorylates BZR1, promoting cytoplasmic retention by 14-3-3 proteins and inactivating its DNA binding

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: BIN2 phosphorylates numerous substrates including BES1 and BZR1

edges.mechanism: Dephosphorylated/active BZR1 in nucleus activates BR-responsive cell elongation genes; bzr1-D constitutively active mutant promotes growth

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRs play a dominant role in regulating cell elongation; BR signaling modulates the functions of cytoskeleton including microtubule and associated proteins as well as actin to control cell elongation
evidence.claim: BZR1 promotes cell elongation and plant height

edges.mechanism: YUC8 (flavin monooxygenase) converts indole-3-pyruvate to IAA (auxin) in the YUC-dependent tryptophan pathway

evidence.title: Auxin-Dependent Cell Elongation During the Shade Avoidance Response
evidence.authors: Ma L, Li G
evidence.evidence_sentence: YUCCA enzymes convert indole-pyruvic acid to indole-3-acetic acid (IAA, auxin), the rate-limiting step in tryptophan-derived auxin biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly binds the YUC8 promoter and activates transcription to drive auxin biosynthesis

evidence.title: Auxin-Dependent Cell Elongation During the Shade Avoidance Response
evidence.authors: Ma L, Li G
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 and PIF5 directly bind to the promoters of auxin biosynthetic genes YUC8, TAA1, and CYP79B2

edges.mechanism: PIF4 activates GA20ox1 transcription to raise GA levels in shade and high temperature

evidence.title: Brassinosteroids: Multidimensional Regulators of Plant Growth, Development, and Stress Responses
evidence.authors: Nolan TM, Vukasinovic N, Liu D, Russinova E, Yin Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 controls expression of GA biosynthetic genes including GA20ox1 to promote elongation under thermomorphogenic conditions

edges.mechanism: Auxin binds TIR1 F-box co-receptor, promoting TIR1 interaction with Aux/IAA repressors and their ubiquitination

evidence.title: Auxin-Dependent Cell Elongation During the Shade Avoidance Response
evidence.authors: Ma L, Li G
evidence.evidence_sentence: Transcript levels of the genes encoding the auxin receptors TRANSPORT INHIBITOR RESPONSE1 (TIR1) and AUXIN SIGNALING F-BOX (AFB) proteins are significantly increased

edges.mechanism: SCF-TIR1 ubiquitinates auxin-bound Aux/IAA19 for 26S proteasome degradation, releasing ARF6/7/8 to activate elongation genes

evidence.title: Auxin-Dependent Cell Elongation During the Shade Avoidance Response
evidence.authors: Ma L, Li G
evidence.evidence_sentence: ARFs released from Aux/IAA repression activate growth genes

edges.mechanism: Aux/IAA IAA19 binds ARF7 transcription factor and represses ARF activity

evidence.title: Auxin-Dependent Cell Elongation During the Shade Avoidance Response
evidence.authors: Ma L, Li G
evidence.evidence_sentence: The arf6 arf7 arf8 triple mutant fails to respond to low R:FR treatment, indicating that ARF6, ARF7, and ARF8 are required for shade-induce cell elongation

edges.mechanism: ARF6 activates auxin-responsive elongation genes in hypocotyl and stem

evidence.title: Auxin-Dependent Cell Elongation During the Shade Avoidance Response
evidence.authors: Ma L, Li G
evidence.evidence_sentence: The arf6 arf7 arf8 triple mutant fails to respond to low R:FR treatment, indicating that ARF6, ARF7, and ARF8 are required for shade-induce cell elongation

edges.mechanism: IPT3 (ATP/ADP isopentenyltransferase 3) is a major CK biosynthesis enzyme producing isopentenyladenine riboside 5'-phosphate from DMAPP and AMP/ADP

evidence.title: Cytokinins
evidence.authors: Kieber JJ, Schaller GE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Cytokinin biosynthesis is controlled by the ATP/ADP isopentenyltransferase (IPT) genes, with seven IPT genes (AtIPT1 and AtIPT3 through AtIPT8) encoding enzymes that synthesize the cytokinin precursor, isopentenyladenine

edges.mechanism: CKX3 (cytokinin oxidase 3) degrades cytokinins; feedback-induced by CK itself

evidence.title: Cytokinins
evidence.authors: Kieber JJ, Schaller GE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Cytokinin degradation is mediated by the cytokinin oxidases, encoded by the CYTOKININ OXIDASE (CKX) gene family; the expression of several CKX genes is induced by cytokinin, providing a feedback mechanism to dampen cytokinin function

edges.mechanism: Auxin represses IPT3 transcription, linking auxin to CK depletion in axillary buds

evidence.title: Cytokinins
evidence.authors: Kieber JJ, Schaller GE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin represses IPT expression in stem tissues leading to decreased CK biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Cytokinin induces CKX3 transcription as homeostatic feedback

evidence.title: Cytokinins
evidence.authors: Kieber JJ, Schaller GE
evidence.evidence_sentence: the expression of several CKX genes is induced by cytokinin, providing a feedback mechanism to dampen cytokinin function

edges.mechanism: CK binds AHK3 receptor

evidence.title: Cytokinins
evidence.authors: Kieber JJ, Schaller GE
evidence.evidence_sentence: [AHK2, AHK3, and AHK4] as cytokinin receptors

edges.mechanism: Activated AHK3 autophosphorylates and transfers phosphate to AHP1, which relays to nucleus

evidence.title: Cytokinins
evidence.authors: Kieber JJ, Schaller GE
evidence.evidence_sentence: There are four major steps in the cytokinin phosphorelay: (i) AHK sensing and signaling, (ii) AHP nuclear translocation, (iii) ARR-dependent transcriptional activation

edges.mechanism: AHP1 phosphotransfer to ARR1 (type-B response regulator) activates its transcriptional activity in nucleus

evidence.title: Cytokinins
evidence.authors: Kieber JJ, Schaller GE
evidence.evidence_sentence: nuclear response regulators (ARRs) that serve as transcriptional regulators

edges.mechanism: Type-B ARRs are positive regulators of cytokinin growth responses; arr1 arr10 arr12 triple mutants have severe growth defects

evidence.title: Cytokinins
evidence.authors: Kieber JJ, Schaller GE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Multiple type-B ARR mutants affect cytokinin responses including inhibition of root elongation, lateral root formation, and hypocotyl elongation

edges.mechanism: ACS (ACC synthase) converts SAM to ACC, the rate-limiting step of ethylene biosynthesis

evidence.title: CTR1 phosphorylates the central regulator EIN2 to control ethylene hormone signaling from the ER membrane to the nucleus in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Ju C, Yoon GM, Shemansky JM, Lin DY, Ying ZI, Chang J, Garrett WM, Kessenbrock M, Groth G, Tucker ML, Cooper B, Kieber JJ, Chang C
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ethylene biosynthesis from SAM proceeds via ACC synthesized by ACS

edges.mechanism: Ethylene binds ETR1 ER receptor and inactivates it; in the absence of ethylene, ETR1 is active and activates CTR1

evidence.title: CTR1 phosphorylates the central regulator EIN2 to control ethylene hormone signaling from the ER membrane to the nucleus in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Ju C, Yoon GM, Shemansky JM, Lin DY, Ying ZI, Chang J, Garrett WM, Kessenbrock M, Groth G, Tucker ML, Cooper B, Kieber JJ, Chang C
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: In the absence of ethylene, ER membrane-located ethylene receptors such as ETHYLENE RESPONSE 1 (ETR1) interact with and activate the Ser/Thr kinase CONSTITUTIVE RESPONSE 1 (CTR1); upon binding of ethylene to the receptors, the activities of both receptors and CTR1 are repressed

edges.mechanism: ETR1 activates CTR1 kinase in the absence of ethylene, maintaining the off-state of ethylene signaling

evidence.title: CTR1 phosphorylates the central regulator EIN2 to control ethylene hormone signaling from the ER membrane to the nucleus in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Ju C, Yoon GM, Shemansky JM, Lin DY, Ying ZI, Chang J, Garrett WM, Kessenbrock M, Groth G, Tucker ML, Cooper B, Kieber JJ, Chang C
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: ER membrane-located ethylene receptors such as ETHYLENE RESPONSE 1 (ETR1) interact with and activate the Ser/Thr kinase CONSTITUTIVE RESPONSE 1 (CTR1)

edges.mechanism: CTR1 phosphorylates EIN2 C-terminus, sequestering it at the ER and preventing EIN3/EIL1 activation

evidence.title: CTR1 phosphorylates the central regulator EIN2 to control ethylene hormone signaling from the ER membrane to the nucleus in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Ju C, Yoon GM, Shemansky JM, Lin DY, Ying ZI, Chang J, Garrett WM, Kessenbrock M, Groth G, Tucker ML, Cooper B, Kieber JJ, Chang C
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: CTR1 phosphorylates EIN2, a key positive component of ethylene signaling. The phosphorylated EIN2 is sequestered and inactivated at the ER membrane

edges.mechanism: Cleaved EIN2 C-terminus translocates to nucleus and stabilizes EIN3 by inhibiting EBF1/EBF2 F-box proteins that otherwise degrade EIN3

evidence.title: CTR1 phosphorylates the central regulator EIN2 to control ethylene hormone signaling from the ER membrane to the nucleus in Arabidopsis
